content.problemStatement:
You are given 222 integers NNN and DDD.

Please construct a tournament graph on NNN vertices such that the diameter is exactly DDD, or report that it is impossible.

A tournament graph is a directed graph such that for every pair of vertices (X,Y)(X, Y)(X,Y) (X≠YX \ne YX=Y), there exists **exactly one** of the edges X→YX \rightarrow YX→Y or Y→XY \rightarrow XY→X. There are no other edges in the graph.

The diameter of a directed graph is defined as the **maximum distance** from node uuu to vvv over all pairs 1≤u,v≤N1 \le u, v \le N1≤u,v≤N. If the graph is not strongly connected, the diameter is considered to be infinity.

The distance is defined as the minimum number of directed edges in a path from uuu to vvv. If there is no such path, the distance is considered to be infinity. Note that the distance from uuu to vvv is not the same as distance from vvv to uuu.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of input contains 222 integers - NNN and DDD.


Output Format
For each test case, do the following:




if there exists a valid tournament with NNN vertices and a diameter of DDD,



print a binary matrix AAA of size N×NN \times NN×N as NNN different strings of size NNN on NNN lines.
A(i,j)=1A_{(i, j)} = 1A(i,j)​=1 if and only if you choose to have the directed edge i→ji \rightarrow ji→j in your tournament graph.
Exactly one of A(i,j)A_{(i, j)}A(i,j)​ and A(j,i)A_{(j, i)}A(j,i)​ should be 111 when i≠ji \ne ji=j to satisfy the tournament property. A(i,i)A_{(i, i)}A(i,i)​ should always be 000.



if there does not exist a valid tournament, print the single integer −1-1−1.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 100010001000

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The tournament graph consists of the edges 1→21 \rightarrow 21→2, 2→32 \rightarrow 32→3 and 3→13 \rightarrow 13→1. We can verify this has a diameter of 222 in the following way. Let d(u,v)d(u, v)d(u,v) denote the distance from node uuu to node vvv.

d(1,1)=d(2,2)=d(3,3)=0d(1, 1) = d(2, 2) = d(3, 3) = 0d(1,1)=d(2,2)=d(3,3)=0
d(1,3)=d(2,1)=d(3,2)=1d(1, 3) = d(2, 1) = d(3, 2) = 1d(1,3)=d(2,1)=d(3,2)=1
d(1,2)=d(2,3)=d(3,1)=2d(1, 2) = d(2, 3) = d(3, 1) = 2d(1,2)=d(2,3)=d(3,1)=2
Hence, the maximum distance is 222.
